en: TO THE CHOIRMASTER: ACCORDING TO THE GITTITH. OF ASAPH. Sing aloud to God our strength; shout for joy to the God of Jacob!
zh: 要向 神我們的力量歡呼， 要向雅各的 神大聲呼叫。

en: Raise a song; sound the tambourine, the sweet lyre with the harp.
zh: 要唱詩歌，打手鼓， 彈奏美好的琴瑟。

en: Blow the trumpet at the new moon, at the full moon, on our feast day.
zh: 要在月朔，在月望， 在我們守節的日子吹角。

en: For it is a statute for Israel, a rule of the God of Jacob.
zh: 因為這是給以色列的律例， 是雅各的 神的典章。

en: He made it a decree in Joseph when he went out over the land of Egypt. I hear a language I had not known:
zh: 約瑟從埃及地出來 的時候， 神為約瑟立了這法規。 我聽見了我不曉得的言語。

en: "I relieved your shoulder of the burden; your hands were freed from the basket.
zh: 神說：“我要除去你 肩頭的重擔， 使你 的手放下筐子。

en: In distress you called, and I delivered you; I answered you in the secret place of thunder; I tested you at the waters of Meribah. Selah
zh: 你在患難中呼求， 我就搭救你； 我在打雷的密雲中回答你， 在米利巴的水邊試驗你。”

en: Hear, O my people, while I admonish you! O Israel, if you would but listen to me!
zh: “我的子民哪！你要聽，我要勸戒你； 以色列啊！但願你肯聽從我。

en: There shall be no strange god among you; you shall not bow down to a foreign god.
zh: 在你中間不可有別的神， 外族人的神你也不可敬拜。

en: I am the LORD your God, who brought you up out of the land of Egypt. Open your mouth wide, and I will fill it.
zh: 我是耶和華你的 神， 曾把你從埃及地領出來。 你要大大張口，我就要給它充滿。

en: "But my people did not listen to my voice; Israel would not submit to me.
zh: 我的子民卻不聽我的話， 以色列不肯服從我。

en: So I gave them over to their stubborn hearts, to follow their own counsels.
zh: 因此，我就任憑他們心裡剛硬， 隨著自己的計謀行事。

en: Oh, that my people would listen to me, that Israel would walk in my ways!
zh: 只要我的子民聽從我， 以色列肯遵行我的道，

en: I would soon subdue their enemies and turn my hand against their foes.
zh: 我就迅速制伏他們的仇敵， 把手轉回來攻擊他們的敵人。

en: Those who hate the LORD would cringe toward him, and their fate would last forever.
zh: 憎恨耶和華的人必向他假意歸順， 他們的刑期必永無止境。

en: But he would feed you with the finest of the wheat, and with honey from the rock I would satisfy you."
zh: 但我 必把上好的麥子給你們 吃， 又用磐石裡的蜂蜜使你們飽足。”
